title: Creamy Coconut Curry Chicken
description: Rich and creamy Thai-style curry made with coconut milk, tender chicken, and aromatic spices. Completely dairy-free comfort food.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Add onion to the pan and sauté 3 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Add curry paste and stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk and fish sauce. Bring to a simmer.
Return chicken to the pan. Add bell peppers and zucchini.
Simmer 15-20 minutes until chicken is cooked through and vegetables are tender.
Stir in lime juice and fresh basil. Serve over rice or cauliflower rice.

title: Avocado Lime Grilled Chicken
description: Juicy grilled chicken topped with fresh avocado-lime salsa. Bright, fresh, and completely dairy-free.
instructions: Mix 2 tbsp lime juice, 2 tbsp olive oil, garlic powder, cumin, 1 tsp salt, and pepper. Marinate chicken for at least 30 minutes.
Make avocado salsa: combine avocados, tomatoes, red onion, cilantro, jalapeño, remaining lime juice, lime zest, and remaining salt. Gently toss.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill chicken 6-7 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Let chicken rest 5 minutes, then top with generous amounts of avocado salsa.
Serve immediately.

title: Dairy-Free Beef Stroganoff
description: Classic beef stroganoff made creamy with coconut cream instead of sour cream. All the comfort, none of the dairy.
instructions: Season beef with salt and pepper. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Brown beef in batches, 2 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil to the skillet. Sauté mushrooms until golden, about 6 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Pour in beef broth and coconut aminos. Bring to a simmer, scraping up browned bits.
Stir in Dijon mustard and thyme. Add arrowroot slurry and stir until sauce thickens.
Reduce heat to low. Stir in coconut cream. Return beef and mushrooms to the pan.
Simmer 5 minutes until heated through. Garnish with parsley. Serve over rice or zucchini noodles.

title: Lemon Herb Baked Salmon
description: Perfectly baked salmon with bright lemon, fresh herbs, and olive oil. Simple, elegant, and naturally dairy-free.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mix olive oil, garlic, lemon zest, dill, parsley, salt, and pepper.
Place salmon fillets on the baking sheet. Brush generously with the herb oil mixture.
Top each fillet with lemon slices.
Bake 12-15 minutes until salmon flakes easily with a fork.
Serve immediately with additional fresh herbs if desired.

title: Thai Coconut Chicken Soup (Tom Kha Gai)
description: Aromatic Thai soup with coconut milk, tender chicken, mushrooms, and fragrant lemongrass. Warming and dairy-free.
instructions: In a large pot, combine chicken broth, coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, lime leaves, and chilies. Bring to a simmer.
Simmer for 10 minutes to infuse flavors.
Add chicken slices and mushrooms. Cook 8-10 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove from heat. Stir in fish sauce and lime juice.
Remove lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves (or leave for presentation but don't eat).
Ladle into bowls. Garnish with fresh cilantro and green onions.

title: 40-Clove Garlic Roasted Chicken
description: Classic French-inspired roasted chicken surrounded by whole garlic cloves that become sweet and spreadable. Naturally dairy-free.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Pat chicken dry and let sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Season chicken inside and out with salt and pepper. Stuff cavity with lemon halves, half the rosemary, and half the thyme.
Rub chicken with olive oil. Place breast-side up in a roasting pan.
Scatter garlic cloves around the chicken. Tuck remaining herbs around the garlic.
Pour chicken broth into the bottom of the pan.
Roast 20 minutes at 425°F, then reduce to 375°F (190°C) and continue roasting 50-60 minutes until internal temp reaches 165°F.
Rest 15 minutes before carving. Serve with soft roasted garlic cloves to spread on the meat.

title: Mediterranean Turkey Meatballs
description: Herb-packed turkey meatballs with sun-dried tomatoes, served with a dairy-free tzatziki made with coconut yogurt.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment.
Mix ground turkey with sun-dried tomatoes, oregano, parsley, garlic, egg, almond flour, 1 tsp salt, and pepper. Form into 24 meatballs.
Arrange on baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil. Bake 22-25 minutes until cooked through.
Make dairy-free tzatziki: combine coconut yogurt, cucumber, dill, lemon juice, 1 minced garlic clove, and remaining salt.
Serve meatballs with tzatziki sauce drizzled over top.

title: Beef and Vegetable Stir-Fry
description: Quick and colorful stir-fry with tender beef, crisp vegetables, and a savory ginger-garlic sauce. Naturally dairy-free.
instructions: Season beef with salt. Heat 2 tbsp avocado oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry beef 2 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry carrots and broccoli 2 minutes. Add bell peppers and snap peas, cook 2 more minutes.
Push vegetables to the side. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds.
Return beef to wok. Add coconut aminos. Toss everything together.
Add arrowroot slurry and cook until sauce thickens, about 1 minute.
Drizzle with sesame oil. Top with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve immediately.

title: Cashew Chicken
description: Better-than-takeout cashew chicken with a savory-sweet sauce, tender chicken, and crunchy cashews.
instructions: Mix coconut aminos, rice vinegar, honey, and chicken broth for the sauce.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat. Season chicken with salt. Stir-fry chicken until golden, about 5 minutes. Remove.
Add remaining oil. Toast cashews for 1 minute until golden. Remove.
Add bell pepper, stir-fry 2 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds.
Return chicken to wok. Pour in sauce. Add arrowroot slurry and cook until sauce thickens.
Add toasted cashews and most of the green onions. Toss to combine.
Serve over rice, topped with remaining green onions.

title: Lamb Kofta Lettuce Wraps
description: Spiced lamb kofta served in crisp lettuce cups with fresh vegetables and a dairy-free tahini sauce.
instructions: Mix ground lamb with grated onion, garlic, parsley, mint, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and 1 tsp salt. Form into small oval patties.
Make tahini sauce: whisk tahini with lemon juice, 3 tbsp water, and remaining salt until smooth.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook kofta patties 3-4 minutes per side until cooked through.
Arrange lettuce cups on a platter. Fill each with kofta, tomatoes, and cucumber.
Drizzle generously with tahini sauce. Serve immediately.

title: Honey Garlic Glazed Salmon
description: Sweet and savory glazed salmon with a caramelized honey-garlic crust. Naturally dairy-free perfection.
instructions: Mix honey, coconut aminos, garlic, ginger, and rice vinegar for the glaze.
Season salmon with salt and pepper.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Place salmon skin-side up and cook 4 minutes until golden.
Flip salmon. Pour glaze around the fish (not directly on top). Cook 3-4 minutes, spooning glaze over the salmon as it cooks.
Continue basting until salmon is cooked through and glaze is sticky and caramelized.
Serve topped with sesame seeds and green onions.

title: Chicken Tikka Skewers
description: Marinated chicken skewers with warm Indian spices. Served with dairy-free coconut raita.
instructions: Mix 0.75 cup coconut yogurt with garam masala, cumin, paprika, turmeric, garlic, ginger, lemon juice, and 1 tsp salt. Add chicken and marinate at least 1 hour.
Make raita: mix remaining coconut yogurt with grated cucumber, mint, and remaining salt.
Thread chicken onto skewers. Preheat grill or broiler to high.
Grill or broil skewers 4-5 minutes per side until chicken is charred and cooked through.
Serve with coconut raita, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Chicken Cauliflower Fried Rice
description: Low-carb fried rice using cauliflower rice, packed with chicken, vegetables, and eggs. Completely dairy-free.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp avocado oil in a wok over high heat. Season chicken with salt and stir-fry until cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove.
Add 1 tbsp oil. Scramble eggs, breaking into small pieces. Remove.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry carrots 2 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds.
Add cauliflower rice. Stir-fry 4-5 minutes until tender but not mushy.
Add peas and cook 1 minute. Return chicken and eggs to the wok.
Add coconut aminos and white pepper. Toss everything together.
Drizzle with sesame oil, top with green onions, and serve.

title: Grilled Steak with Chimichurri
description: Perfectly grilled steak topped with vibrant, herbaceous chimichurri sauce. A naturally dairy-free classic.
instructions: Make chimichurri: Finely chop parsley, cilantro, oregano, and garlic (or pulse in food processor). Mix with olive oil, vinegar, red pepper flakes, and 0.5 tsp salt. Let sit at least 15 minutes.
Remove steaks from refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking. Pat dry and season generously with remaining salt and pepper.
Preheat grill to high heat.
Grill steaks 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temp 130°F).
Rest steaks 10 minutes. Slice against the grain.
Serve drizzled generously with chimichurri sauce.

title: Coconut Lime Chicken Thighs
description: Crispy-skinned chicken thighs in a creamy coconut lime sauce with fresh cilantro. Restaurant-quality and dairy-free.
instructions: Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, paprika, and cumin.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Place chicken skin-side down and cook 7-8 minutes until skin is golden and crispy. Flip and cook 3 more minutes.
Remove chicken. Add garlic to the pan and cook 30 seconds.
Pour in coconut milk, lime juice, and lime zest. Stir and bring to a simmer.
Return chicken to the pan, skin-side up. Transfer to 400°F oven and bake 15-18 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with sauce spooned over.

title: Classic Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce. A naturally dairy-free Middle Eastern breakfast favorite.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper. Cook 8 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, cumin, paprika, cayenne, salt, and pepper. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes. Stir and simmer 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Make 6 wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each well.
Cover and cook 5-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Sprinkle with fresh parsley. Serve directly from the skillet with crusty bread or on its own.

title: Sweet Potato Turkey Chili
description: Hearty, warming chili with ground turkey, sweet potatoes, and beans. Naturally dairy-free comfort food.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown ground turkey, breaking it up, about 6 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add onion and bell pepper. Cook 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Add chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt. Stir for 30 seconds.
Add sweet potatoes, diced tomatoes, black beans, and chicken broth. Stir well.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes until sweet potatoes are tender.
Serve topped with avocado slices and fresh cilantro.

title: Balsamic Glazed Salmon
description: Rich balsamic glaze over perfectly seared salmon. Elegant, flavorful, and naturally dairy-free.
instructions: Whisk balsamic vinegar, honey, garlic, and Dijon mustard in a small bowl.
Season salmon with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Place salmon skin-side up and cook 4 minutes.
Flip salmon. Pour balsamic glaze around the fish. Cook 4-5 minutes, spooning glaze over salmon frequently.
The glaze will reduce and become syrupy. Remove when salmon is cooked through.
Serve salmon drizzled with remaining glaze and topped with fresh rosemary.

title: Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: Aromatic Moroccan stew with chicken, preserved lemons, olives, and warming spices. Naturally dairy-free.
instructions: Season chicken with salt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cinnamon.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown chicken on all sides, about 8 minutes total. Remove.
Add onions and cook 8 minutes until golden. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Add chicken broth and saffron. Stir to combine.
Return chicken to the pot. Add preserved lemon and olives. Bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook on low heat for 40-45 minutes until chicken is very tender.
Garnish with fresh cilantro. Serve over couscous or rice.

title: Teriyaki Beef Bowls
description: Sweet and savory teriyaki beef over rice with fresh vegetables. A dairy-free takeout favorite.
instructions: Mix coconut aminos, honey, rice vinegar, garlic, and ginger for teriyaki sauce.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over high heat. Stir-fry beef until browned, about 3 minutes.
Pour teriyaki sauce over beef. Cook 2-3 minutes until sauce thickens and coats the beef.
Drizzle with sesame oil.
Divide rice among bowls. Top with teriyaki beef, steamed broccoli, and shredded carrots.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.

title: Greek Lemon Chicken Soup (Avgolemono)
description: Silky, lemony chicken soup thickened with eggs instead of cream. Bright, comforting, and dairy-free.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Add chicken broth, rice, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil.
Add whole chicken breasts. Reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes until chicken is cooked and rice is tender.
Remove chicken and shred. Return to pot.
Whisk eggs with lemon juice and zest in a bowl. Slowly ladle 1 cup hot broth into the egg mixture while whisking constantly (this tempers the eggs).
Remove pot from heat. Slowly pour egg mixture into the soup while stirring.
Stir in fresh dill. Serve immediately (don't boil after adding eggs).

title: Coconut Crusted Cod
description: Crispy coconut-crusted cod fillets with a sweet chili dipping sauce. Tropical and completely dairy-free.
instructions: Make sweet chili sauce: whisk honey, rice vinegar, red pepper flakes, garlic, and 1 tbsp water. Set aside.
Season cod with salt, pepper, and lime juice.
Set up breading station: almond flour in one bowl, beaten eggs in another, shredded coconut in a third.
Dredge each fillet in almond flour, then egg, then press into coconut to coat.
Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook cod 3-4 minutes per side until golden and fish flakes easily.
Serve with sweet chili dipping sauce.

title: Dairy-Free Stuffed Peppers
description: Colorful bell peppers stuffed with seasoned ground turkey, rice, and vegetables. No cheese needed for maximum flavor.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Cut tops off peppers and remove seeds.
Heat olive oil in a skillet. Cook onion 5 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Add ground turkey, breaking it up. Season with Italian seasoning, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook until browned.
Stir in cooked rice, diced tomatoes, and half the tomato sauce. Mix well.
Stuff each pepper with the turkey mixture. Place in a baking dish.
Pour remaining tomato sauce over peppers. Add 1/4 cup water to the dish.
Cover with foil and bake 35 minutes. Uncover and bake 10 more minutes.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve.
